title: Xenial Tuscan Fish Flatbread
description: A grassy, cheese-pull Tuscan flatbread topped with sashimi-cut salmon in a rich fish stock, served in a lacquerware bowl. The dish balances fresh arugula, rosemary, and a drizzle of olive oil for a monsoon-inspired flavor.
instructions: Preheat oven to 430°F and place a sheet of parchment on a baking tray.
Roll out the focaccia dough into a 12-inch circle, brush the top with olive oil, and rub minced garlic over the surface.
Bake the dough for 12 minutes until lightly golden.
While the bread is baking, bring the fish stock to a simmer and add rosemary, sea salt, and pepper.
Slice the salmon fillet into sashimi-style thin pieces and gently fold them into the warmed stock.
After the bread is ready, place it on a lacquerware bowl and spoon the hot salmon stock mixture over the top.
Sprinkle shredded mozzarella, grated pecorino, capers, and a handful of arugula over the sauce.
Return the flatbread to the oven for 3 minutes to melt the cheeses, then serve immediately.
